data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4b e308: Claim-gate <-> plan-review ref-resolution coherence, shared boundary + ADR 0002); audit against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. 4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 THIS, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). The union of the eight children covers every parent criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

WHAT THIS CHILD IS: S4b is the slice that was carved out to own EXACTLY the seam the standing G4 dependency-gap (filed on S4 30b3) identified — the claim-gate freshness re-check and plan-review file_impact hashing must resolve the ref through ONE shared boundary at the SAME pinned-SHA basis, rather than two independently-coded resolution sites that can diverge (whole-HEAD vs pinned-SHA) and re-introduce the staleness false-positive ADR 0002 set out to prevent. It is a coherence/consolidation slice, not a primary owner of any parent AC; it REINFORCES parent AC1 (snapshot-only reads) and AC2 (reproducible signed verdict) by closing the divergence risk in the attestation hashing path.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role + bucket):
- AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). Bucket STRUCTURAL/REINFORCING w.r.t. this child: its AC1 (plan-review file_impact hashing + claim-gate freshness re-check resolve the ref through ONE shared boundary at the same pinned-SHA basis — not two hash sites) closes the specific attestation-hashing path of AC1 that S3's gate-file-tool re-rooting does not reach. Not an absence (S1/S3 own the bulk; this child consolidates the residual hashing seam).
- AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY by S4 (30b3) as primary owner atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. Bucket REINFORCING w.r.t. this child: a reproducible verdict requires that the SIGNED dependency map (dependency_hashes) and the later claim-gate freshness re-check hash at the SAME basis; this child's AC1/AC2 (one shared boundary + a negative test asserting both gates hash at the SAME ref, guarding whole-HEAD divergence) is what makes the bound dependency map reproducible rather than re-divergent on re-check. Not an absence (S4 owns the signing act).
- AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local documented back-out) — SPLIT across S3 (reads in-place) + S4 (cannot sign) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs without git-lock contention / cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim under a free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 + S2 + S2b. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure -> descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (portable macOS+Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (impl) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY by S2/S2b + S3 + S6. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives: PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — PARTIALLY co-owned: the architecture ADR (content-addressed layout, flock GC interlock, read-tree/checkout-index, PID-lease rejection) is S2; the manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision is S4. THIS child carries ADR 0002 (the code-drift-invalidation ADR) and its AC3 updates ADR 0002 to record the shared-resolution consolidation + manifest-channel cross-ref. ADR 0002 is a DIFFERENT ADR from the architecture ADR named in parent AC8; this child's ADR-0002 update is adjacent documentation, not the parent-AC8 architecture-ADR obligation (which S2/S4 own). Either way parent AC8 is covered by S2/S4. Not an absence.
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this is S4b, the 8th enumerated story) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child. (Note: this child's existence is itself part of satisfying the parent's explicit "EIGHT enumerated child stories" count — S4b is the 6th bullet / b73c+e308 being the two added beyond the original six.)

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3/AC8. This child's contribution is the coherence guarantee under "reproducible ... never the server's mutable checkout": without the single shared ref-resolution boundary, the claim-gate freshness re-check (attest.py:325-326) re-hashes from _config.repo_root (the mutable checkout) while the signed map was bound at the pinned SHA — exactly the moving-checkout divergence the success criteria forbid. This child closes that residual path. No uncovered obligation introduced; the obligations it touches (AC1/AC2 reproducibility + ref-coherence) are ALSO claimed by S1/S3/S4 and are not orphaned.

Named-consumer / sibling-delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- The attested-signing manifest pin is explicitly delegated OUT of this child to sibling S4 (30b3) ("Out: the attested-signing manifest pin (sibling story)"); S4 is present in the roster and owns it (its AC1/AC2). Clean split — S4 binds the SHA; this child consolidates the resolution boundary the bound map and the re-check share.
- snapshot/cache (S1/S2) explicitly out of this child's scope; S1/S2/S2b own them.
- This child's negative test (AC2: assert both gates hash at the SAME ref) is its own deliverable, in-scope, not delegated.

ANTI-FP cross-check on the one plausible absence: could a parent criterion be left UNCOVERED because this child carves ref-coherence away from S4 but neither lands the routing into attest.py? Checked against the whole roster — NO parent AC is orphaned: parent AC1's snapshot-only reads are claimed by S1+S3; AC2 reproducibility by S4; and the ref-coherence sub-obligation is now explicitly CLAIMED by THIS child's AC1. A criterion claimed by a child is covered for G3 purposes; "the criterion is claimed but the file_impact may name the wrong/insufficient module (signing.py vs. the actual hashing sites in plan_review/attest.py)" is a CONSISTENCY/dependency-gap question (G4) about WHICH file the shared boundary lands in and WHO routes it, not a G3 union-coverage absence.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap thread on S4 (30b3) observes that the three sign/verify-side hashing sites (attest.py:106-107 dependency_hashes, :191-196 _rehash, :325-326 the ADR-0002 freshness re-check) all root on _config.repo_root, and that THIS child's file_impact lists src/rebar/signing.py + docs/adr/0002 but NOT src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py — i.e. the shared boundary is claimed but the hashing sites that must consume it are not named in any child's file_impact. That is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about whether this child's stated file_impact actually reaches the hashing path it claims in AC1 — it belongs in the G4 channel for this pairing, not as a G3 union-coverage absence, because the ref-coherence obligation IS claimed by a child (this one's AC1).

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the claim-gate<->plan-review ref-resolution coherence slice (one shared resolution boundary consumed by both the plan-review file_impact hashing and the claim-gate freshness re-check, a negative test asserting same-ref hashing, the ADR 0002 consolidation update, and a documented reversible back-out). It REINFORCES parent AC1 (snapshot-only reads in the attestation hashing path) and AC2 (reproducible signed verdict / no whole-HEAD divergence) and is the 8th enumerated child satisfying parent AC9's story count. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
